content: The intrinsic reward signals from curiosity-driven RL methods (ICM, RND) can be repurposed as a dynamic quality filter for active learning agent memory, where only memories that trigger above-threshold curiosity are retained in the limited-capacity SOTA active memory buffer to optimize long-horizon planning. This framework creates a natural tradeoff between memory efficiency and exploratory utility that outperforms fixed recency or importance-based retention rules.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
